TextENGNew: ``...What's the matter?@k@vS13/04/DS41040049. You're acting rather odd this morning, Rika.``
``...Is that so?@k@vS13/05/DS41050115. How do I seem odd?``
``I-I can't quite put it into words, but...... you seem... more grown-up, somehow.``
``...Au au au.@k@vS13/12/DS41070130. Rika is a grown-up, you know.@k@vS13/12/DS41070131. Au au au.``

``Hanyuu, no teasing today.@k@vS13/05/DS41050117. ...Satoko, you too, please don't fool around.@k@vS13/05/DS41050118. I'm not in the mood for it.@k@vS13/05/DS41050119. ...Starting today, we have about one week.``
Satoko gave me a puzzled look.
She probably thought something was wrong with me physically.
...I do intend to talk to Keiichi and everyone, Satoko included, but... I don't know how to bring it up.
Everyone loves to joke around, so if they take it as a joke, it's all over.
...How can I get them to take my situation seriously...?
``Good morning, Chie-sensei!@k@vS13/04/DS41040052. Oh, you're here too, Coach.@k@vS13/04/DS41040053. Good morning!``

``Good morning, Satoko-chan and Rika-san.@k@vS13/10/DS41100002. Oh my?@k@|@y@vS13/10/DS41100003. I don't see a maid headband on you today?@k@vS13/10/DS41100004. That's a violation of the dress code! Punishment, punishment~~~!!!``
``Dr. Irie!@k@vS13/22/VT4A_chie1010. Please stop that kind of behavior at school!``
``Coach.@k@vS13/04/DS41040055. Rika hasn't been herself this morning.@k@vS13/04/DS41040056. Could you take a look at her, please?``
``...Oh? What seems to be the matter?@k@vS13/10/DS41100006. Did you catch a cold?``
......I still don't know how to broach the subject with my friends, but Irie is already on our side.
Maybe I can speak to him directly.
At this rate, the whole day will slip away while I agonize over how to word things.
...Then I'll start with Irie.
Hanyuu nodded approvingly, as if to say that was a good idea.
``Au au, Satoko, Satoko, let's set up a prank at Keiichi's desk before he gets here.``
``Oh ho ho ho!!@k@vS13/04/DS41040058. It's not a prank, please! It's a trap! Use the proper term!@k@vS13/04/DS41040059. I shall instruct you in the fine art of anti-Keiichi traps!``
``Au au au au auuu!``
Hanyuu and Satoko cheerfully ran off to the classroom.
...I think Hanyuu did that on purpose to give me an opening.
Then I mustn't waste this kindness and this opportunity.
``...Irie, this isn't the best place. Could we talk in the nurse's office?``
``Of course, that's fine.@k@vS13/10/DS41100008. Let's head there.``
Irie must have assumed this was about something related to Hinamizawa Syndrome, and readily agreed to move somewhere private.
Even the slightest change in my condition could be a harbinger of something catastrophic.
Even if it were just a cold, Irie treats any shift in my health with the utmost sensitivity.
``What's wrong?@k@vS13/10/DS41100010. Is something the matter with your health?``
``...Irie.@k@vS13/05/DS41050122. I'm going to say something strange, but I need you to listen seriously.@k@vS13/05/DS41050123. This is an extremely serious matter.``
I fixed Irie with the most earnest expression I had ever shown him.
Irie usually acts frivolous, but he knows how to switch to being serious when the situation calls for it.
He read my seriousness and adopted a suitably grave expression in return.
...Now then... how do I begin?
``...Irie.@k@vS13/05/DS41050125. I'm what you call the queen carrier, correct?``
``Yes.@k@vS13/10/DS41100012. The parasite within you, Rika-san, is unique, different from anyone else in the village.``
``...And if I were to die, what would happen?``
``......As I've explained before, something catastrophic would occur.@k@vS13/10/DS41100014. ...No, to be precise, something catastrophic is 'believed to' occur, would be more accurate.``
``After all, it's impossible to verify without you actually dying.``
``...However, in cases of social brain parasites similar to Hinamizawa Syndrome, the death of the queen carrier has triggered mass suicides and riots on multiple occasions.``
``...That area falls under Takano-san's expertise.@k@vS13/10/DS41100018. You'd be better off asking her directly.@k@vS13/10/DS41100019. It's not really my field.``
...In other words, the person who best understands what catastrophe my death would bring is none other than@k Takano herself.
...Which makes it all the more baffling why Takano would want to kill me...
Obviously, I'm not going to figure this out on my own.
Irie is right here in front of me.
Maybe he'll know something I don't.
...I should ask before drowning in my own thoughts.
``...Irie.@k@vS13/05/DS41050128. Please just answer without interrupting.@k@vS13/05/DS41050129. ...Is there anyone who would stand to gain from killing me?``
``What?@k@vS13/10/DS41100021. Kill you, Rika-san?!@k@vS13/10/DS41100022. Who would...!``
``Irie, you promised to answer without interrupting, remember?``
``......Right, sorry.@k@vS13/10/DS41100024. ...No, I can't imagine anyone.@k@vS13/10/DS41100025. There shouldn't be a single person who would benefit from your death.``
``To reiterate, if anything were to happen to the queen carrier, every infected person would reach terminal onset within 48 hours.``

``That is arguably the most dangerous aspect of Hinamizawa Syndrome.@k@vS13/10/DS41100028. It's a catastrophe on a scale incomparable to individual symptoms like paranoid delusions or psychotic breaks.``
``...Would anyone profit from triggering that catastrophe?``
``Absolutely not.@k@vS13/10/DS41100030. Killing you is tantamount to killing all two thousand villagers.@k@vS13/10/DS41100031. It's something no one would consider, not even in jest.``

``...If I die, everyone in Hinamizawa will be consumed by paranoid delusions, go mad, perhaps even slaughter one another.@k@vS13/05/DS41050133. ......What a fitting finale for a village of descendants who carry the blood of demons.``
changeReason: New translation adds 'consumed by paranoid delusions' (JPN 被害妄想に取り憑かれて = 'possessed by persecution complex/paranoid delusions'), a key detail the original omitted.

TextENGNew: ...Could that be what Takano is actually after...?
Everyone knows Takano has a certain fascination with cruelty, given the disturbing things she's always spouting.
She digs through the dark history of Onigafuchi Village and has let slip that she'd love to witness the savagery of the man-eating demons with her own eyes.
Even if she were trying to make that a reality by killing me and driving every villager to terminal onset, it wouldn't be entirely unthinkable...
``Of course, that is the worst-case scenario we envision.@k@vS13/10/DS41100033. ...Which is why, should anything happen to you, a certain emergency protocol is designed to be activated.``
``...An emergency protocol?@k@vS13/05/DS41050135. That's the first I've heard of it.``

``I imagine this is the first time I've mentioned it.@k@vS13/10/DS41100035. ...It's a rather disturbing topic, so I'd avoided bringing it up.@k@vS13/10/DS41100036. The Irie Institute's highest priority in treating Hinamizawa Syndrome is the protection of the queen carrier.``
``However, in the event the queen carrier dies and a mass acute onset among all carriers is anticipated, an emergency countermeasure is to be swiftly executed.``
...A reasonable precaution. If two thousand villagers went berserk, there would be no guarantee the damage would stay contained within the village.
If such a scenario could be foreseen, it was only natural to prepare countermeasures in advance.

``There exist a set of emergency manuals for crisis situations,@k@vS13/10/DS41100039. and the one designated for the most critical scenario, that is, in the event of your death, is called Emergency Manual No. 34.``
``...And what happens when it's activated?``

``Before the villagers can descend into madness, ...emergency measures are swiftly carried out on all of them.@k@vS13/10/DS41100041. Specifically, a specialized JSDF unit would seal off Hinamizawa and ensure that everything is treated as though it 'never happened'...``
``.........That too, ...is the first I've heard of it...``
``I'm sorry.@k@vS13/10/DS41100043. It wasn't something I liked talking about either...``
Until now, I'd always been concerned about what happened before my death, never what came after.
Whatever happened afterward was someone else's problem.
But to think that such a thing had been happening every single time I died...
No, that I had been causing it to happen...!
If the enemy has the power to wipe an entire village off the map, then snuffing out one little girl like me would be child's play.
...Hanyuu told me to consult everyone, but... can we really stand against an opponent like that...?
And then this emergency manual demolished the motive I thought I'd just pieced together for Takano.
I had imagined she wanted the villagers to go mad, literally becoming demons, tearing each other apart in a hellish frenzy.
If witnessing that was her motive, I could at least understand it. But according to Irie, it wouldn't even come to that.
changeReason: Original heavily truncated: 'But according to what Irie said, that wouldn't even happen' omits the first half of JPN which discusses the motive of wanting to witness the aftermath (それを鑑賞したいというのが動機なら理解もできたが).

TextENGNew: Before any of that could happen, the emergency measures would be enacted on all the villagers, and everything would be treated as though it 'never happened'...
changeReason: Original drastically truncated: just 'The villagers will be all killed before it does.' New translation captures the JPN meaning that emergency measures would be enacted and everything would be treated as though it 'never happened.'

TextENGNew: ...I'm not Takano, so I can't say for certain, but... I don't think that outcome would satisfy her.
Killing me for that purpose seems like a stretch, considering how long we've known each other across all these dizzying repetitions.
``...Could Takano and the Mountain Dogs actually kill me? Is that something that's possible?``

``That's absurd...!@k@vS13/10/DS41100045. It's absolutely impossible.@k@vS13/10/DS41100046. If anything, Takano-san is the most conscientious about your protection, and one of the Mountain Dogs' primary missions is to guard you.@k@vS13/10/DS41100047. Something like that is simply unthinkable.``
``...Irie.@k@vS13/05/DS41050140. Even so, if Takano were to kill me, what motive could you imagine?``
``I-I can't think of one.@k@vS13/10/DS41100049. She's the one who understands the dangers surrounding the queen carrier better than anyone.``

``...Besides, you know how she is.@k@vS13/10/DS41100051. She pours extraordinary passion into her research on Hinamizawa Syndrome.@k@vS13/10/DS41100052. Killing you would directly contradict that passion.``
``...But, Irie.@k@vS13/05/DS41050142. The plan is to eradicate Hinamizawa Syndrome within three years, isn't it?``
``Eradication means destroying the syndrome entirely.@k@vS13/05/DS41050144. ...For someone who wants to keep researching, how does that sit with her...?``
``Hmm, ......well...@k@vS13/10/DS41100054. ...For me, Hinamizawa Syndrome is something to be cured. But for Takano-san, it's research material.``

``...In fact, she was strongly opposed to the three-year plan, and I've heard she even went to Tokyo alone to petition the board of directors for an indefinite extension.@k@vS13/10/DS41100056. Though it seems that effort ended in failure.``
``...So what you're saying is... Takano wants to continue her research, but the syndrome will be eradicated within three years... meaning Takano will lose her research subject entirely...?``
``......One could certainly put it that way.``
Just faintly... I think I'm starting to glimpse something.
The Irie Institute is by no means a small organization.
It employs a considerable number of people and operates on an enormous budget.
That level of investment is necessary just to research Hinamizawa Syndrome.
......Which means that once the Institute has declared it will wrap up the research in three years, there's nothing Takano can do about it no matter how much she personally objects.
``...If the research is going to be terminated anyway, could she want to flip the whole table in one final, spectacular act...?``
``Th-That's absurd...!@k@vS13/10/DS41100059. Something like that must never happen...!``
``But you know Takano's nature, don't you?@k@vS13/05/DS41050148. She would do it. She's capable of killing me... isn't that possible...?``
``...Impossible...@k@vS13/10/DS41100061. It can't be...@k@vS13/10/DS41100062. Something that terrifying is... absolutely...``
I felt as though I'd caught the faintest glimpse of Takano's motive's tail... but something tells me there's more to it.
After all, the research will continue for at least three more years.
During that time, Takano's position could be reconsidered and the research extended.@k At the very least, three years of research are still guaranteed.
So why would she become desperate now, in 1983, when she still has a full three years left?
Besides, I'm always killed in June 1983.
The exact date may shift slightly, but it almost never changes months.
...That speaks to a firm, unwavering intent.
If the research were being axed immediately, that would be one thing, but to grow desperate in 1983 with three years still remaining, the motive seems too weak for that.
There must be a stronger reason why it has to be June 1983...

Beyond that, no matter how many times I pressed Irie about the possibility of Takano killing me, he just kept repeating 'that's absurd' and 'impossible,' and didn't seem likely to be of much more help.
If I could clearly explain Takano's motive, he would probably become my ally...
For now, this seemed to be the limit of what I could accomplish with Irie.
...I'll try having the same conversation with Tomitake.
Tomitake is far more closely connected to 'Tokyo' than Irie.
He might be able to tell me things Irie couldn't.
...If I can learn some critical fact from him and construct a convincing enough motive for why Takano would threaten my life, that would be ideal...
Tomitake should already be visiting Hinamizawa at this time of year.
He's often with Takano, but he also frequently wanders around on his own, indulging his hobby of photographing wild birds.
I'll catch him when Takano isn't around and try talking to him.
...Though Tomitake does harbor strong feelings for Takano.
...I'm worried he won't take this as seriously as Irie did...
``...Thank you.@k@vS13/05/DS41050150. I'm going back to the classroom. If you learn anything about Takano, please let me know.``
``R-Rika-san...!@k@vS13/10/DS41100064. Why do you think Takano-san is trying to kill you...?!@k@vS13/10/DS41100065. It can't be... acute onset...?!``
``...If you suspect that, shall we run a quick test right now?``
I'd sprung some alarming things on him out of nowhere, so I couldn't blame Irie for suspecting that.
But proving otherwise was easy, thanks to the rapid diagnostic syringe Irie had developed by this point.
I had to submit to the injection and demonstrate that I hadn't entered terminal onset.
The result was negative.
Obviously.
``......For Takano-san to... it's impossible... just impossible...``
Perhaps just making Irie aware that Takano might try to kill me was a gain in itself.
I left the nurse's office.
...I could only hope that Irie, now curious about Takano, would look into things on his own...
``...How did it go, Rika?@k@vS13/12/DS41070135. Did you learn anything useful?``
``Yes and no.@k@vS13/05/DS41050153. ...It's hard to say. But I did learn a few interesting things.``

``First, the Irie Institute is planning to eradicate Hinamizawa Syndrome within three years.@k@vS13/05/DS41050155. That's something Takano, who wants to continue her research, would find deeply unpleasant.``

``She went to Tokyo alone to plead with the 'Tokyo' people for the continuation of her research, but was turned down.@k@vS13/05/DS41050157. ...That rejection might have driven her to something desperate.``

``The second thing is more interesting. There's something ominous called Emergency Manual No. 34.@k@vS13/05/DS41050159. If I die, it's executed, and emergency measures are carried out across the entire village.``
``...Au au au au...``
``They say that when I die, all the villagers will reach terminal onset, right?``
``At first I thought Takano was killing me because she wanted to see that happen. But since this emergency manual exists, the village would be wiped out before that spectacle could even unfold.``
``...So that probably isn't her motive either.@k@vS13/05/DS41050163. No matter how much I asked Irie, all he'd say was 'that's absurd.'``
``According to him, Takano understands the gravity of the situation better than anyone.@k@vS13/05/DS41050165. Therefore, she has the least reason of all to kill me.``
``I know.@k@vS13/05/DS41050167. I've only asked Irie so far.@k@vS13/05/DS41050168. There's still a chance we can get information or ideas from other people.``
``That's right.@k@vS13/12/DS41070139. Unless we bring everyone's strength together, a miracle won't happen.``
``Without a miracle, we can't possibly win against an enemy like that.``
``...Lunch break is almost here.@k@vS13/12/DS41070141. I think that will be a good chance to talk to everyone.``
``That said...@k@vS13/05/DS41050171. The problem is how to get them to listen seriously...``

``The moment I start talking about a secret organization called 'Tokyo,' they'll obviously say 'what manga is this from?'@k@vS13/05/DS41050173. ...I need to think of a way to present it that avoids misunderstanding.``
``...Au.@k@vS13/12/DS41070143. I think it'll actually go better if you don't overthink it and just tell them straightforwardly.``

``Yes.@k@vS13/12/DS41070145. Just try it.@k@vS13/12/DS41070146. If you say it the way I suggest, they won't just listen without teasing, they'll take it incredibly seriously and brainstorm with you!``
I gave her a skeptical look, but Hanyuu said it as though it were the most obvious thing in the world.
For all her faults, Hanyuu has spent a long time observing all sorts of people.
Even though she'd never spoken directly with Mion before this life, she knows Mion better than I do.
Following the approach Hanyuu taught me, I brought the matter to everyone during lunch break...
``Hmm hmm...!@k@vS13/03/DS41030085. Now that's interesting!@k@vS13/03/DS41030086. What happens next?!``
``Hauuu!@k@vS13/02/DS41020087. Rika-chan, that's amazing!@k@vS13/02/DS41020088. How do you come up with things like that?!``
``Right?!@k@vS13/01/DS41010089. That's a surprising talent you've got there.@k@vS13/01/DS41010090. Polish it up a bit more and you could have a pretty great manga on your hands!``

``I'm flabbergasted!@k@vS13/04/DS41040061. You seemed out of sorts since this morning, so I thought you had some terrible problem!@k@vS13/04/DS41040062. If it was something like this, you should have come to me first!``
``...See?@k@vS13/12/DS41070148. Everyone is incredibly reliable.@k@vS13/12/DS41070149. Au au!``
``R-...Reliable, you say.@k@vS13/05/DS41050176. ...It really is all in how you say it...``

``Believe it or not, this old man can handle everything from circle applications to storyboards and rough drafts,@k@vS13/03/DS41030088. inking, screen tones, backgrounds, typesetting, proofreading, printing, publishing, distribution, and support!@|@y@k@vS13/03/DS41030089. Coming to me was a very wise choice!``
``Au au!@k@vS13/12/DS41070151. Mion is amazing!@k@vS13/12/DS41070152. She can be the manga artist, the editor, the publisher, and the bookstore all rolled into one!``
``Seriously?!@k@vS13/01/DS41010092. My old man says stuff like that too.@k@vS13/01/DS41010093. Is doing everything yourself some kind of trend?``
``Hauuu...@k@vS13/02/DS41020090. Rena is more curious about what genre Mii-chan would write in, I'm curious!``
changeReason: Subject changed: original says 'I'm curious about what kind of comic' but JPN says レナはそんなことより魅ぃちゃんのジャンルが気になる ('Rena is more curious about Mii-chan's genre'); Rena speaks in third person here.

TextENGNew: ``Now, now!@k@vS13/04/DS41040064. You're all getting sidetracked!@k@|@y@vS13/04/DS41040065. Let's focus on helping Rika!``
...I told them candidly about the situation surrounding Hinamizawa.
Framing it as a manga I wanted to draw, asking for help fleshing out the setting.
If I'd just laid out the situation plainly, they might have questioned my sanity. But to see such a small change in framing make this much difference...
...Hanyuu, you're pretty clever.
``The key point is that if the queen carrier dies, the emergency measure kicks in and the entire village is wiped out,@k@vS13/01/DS41010095. right?``
``Goodness, if such a person really existed, ...she'd need round-the-clock protection!@k@vS13/04/DS41040067. One bump on the head from a tofu corner and the whole village is gone!``
``We should just wrap her up tight and lock her in a storehouse!``
``But she gets killed... right?@k@vS13/02/DS41020092. If they know her death will cause a catastrophe, why would they kill her? Why?``

``......Exactly.@k@vS13/05/DS41050178. The villain at the secret research facility knows what will happen, yet still tries to kill m-- ...no, the queen carrier.@k@vS13/05/DS41050179. But I can't think of a convincing motive.``
...I can almost see the faint outline of the motive, only for it to slip away again...
Can they really find a clue without knowing any of the actual details...?
``Wa ha ha ha!@k@vS13/03/DS41030091. That's a piece of cake!@k@vS13/03/DS41030092. The fact alone that killing the queen destroys the whole village is plenty of motive!``
Mion laughed airily and swept aside the question I'd been racking my brain over since last night.
``...But Mion.@k@vS13/12/DS41070154. This villain wants to continue researching the disease, so there's no way she'd bring about its end herself.@k@vS13/12/DS41070155. Au au.``
``That's not a big deal at all.@k@vS13/03/DS41030094. Just give the villain an even bigger puppet master pulling her strings.@k@vS13/03/DS41030095. Or rather, an even worse villain who's using her.``
A villain... who's using Takano...?

``Listen.@k@vS13/03/DS41030097. If the queen dies, the village plunges into chaos.@k@vS13/03/DS41030098. To cover it up, a massive emergency operation is carried out and everything is swept under the rug.``
``This is fundamentally a last-resort countermeasure against the worst-case scenario, a trump card that must never be played.``
``If it is played, sure, the immediate crisis might be contained, but the fallout itself would be catastrophic!``
``Hmm!@k@vS13/01/DS41010097. True. In the world of politics, it always comes down to who takes responsibility and who has to resign.``
``If an operation that kills thousands of people is actually carried out, someone will definitely have to answer for allowing it to happen.``
``Well, since it's a secret operation, the fallout won't be public. But there will absolutely be a fierce internal reckoning within the 'Tokyo' organization!``
``Which means there's more than enough motive for killing the queen carrier right there.``
``...Au?@k@vS13/12/DS41070157. How so?@k@vS13/12/DS41070158. Who could possibly benefit from such chaos...?``
``Plenty of people! Of course there are!@k@vS13/03/DS41030104. Think about it.@k@vS13/03/DS41030105. The wind always blows in one direction.``
``That wind is a headwind for some, but for those facing the opposite direction, it's a tailwind!@k@vS13/03/DS41030107. No matter what happens, there are always people who lose and people who gain.``
``...I see.@k@vS13/04/DS41040070. Oh ho ho!@k@vS13/04/DS41040071. My trap-oriented brain is positively buzzing!``

``In other words, if a disaster of that scale occurs, certain people will be held accountable.@k@vS13/04/DS41040073. And for those who oppose them, that would be an extremely lucrative turn of events, wouldn't it?!``

``That's right.@k@vS13/02/DS41020094. Every organization has competing factions with different agendas.@k@vS13/02/DS41020095. Especially a massive shadow organization like 'Tokyo.' There must be all sorts of people with conflicting schemes.``
``In other words, for anyone looking to climb over their rivals,@k@vS13/01/DS41010100. assassinating the queen carrier and triggering a crisis would be well worth it.``

``...S-So you're saying that even if Taka... I mean, even if the villain herself has no reason,@k@vS13/12/DS41070160. there could be people within 'Tokyo' who do have a reason to kill the queen carrier?``
changeReason: Number changed: original lacks 'the villain herself' distinction; new translation adds 'herself' and restructures to match JPN distinction between the villain personally and the many people within 'Tokyo'.

TextENGNew: ``Heh heh heh!@k@vS13/03/DS41030109. Exactly!@k@|@y@vS13/03/DS41030110. And here's the backstory for the villain at the research facility!@k@vS13/03/DS41030111. She wants to keep her research going, but the decision to shut it down in three years has already been made, right?``

``So this researcher is watching everything she's worked on be reduced to nothing.@k@vS13/03/DS41030113. That means the achievements she spent half her life building are about to be destroyed. She must be in tremendous anguish.``
``And it's not just that.@k@vS13/03/DS41030115. Her pride must be deeply wounded too.``

``Right.@k@vS13/01/DS41010102. If the research this villain devoted half her life to isn't recognized by her superiors, and they tell her to wrap it up in three years, it would feel like her entire existence was being denied.``
``That must be incredibly painful.@k@vS13/02/DS41020097. Having everything you've built up over a lifetime be rejected like that... it must hurt so much...``
``...Y-...Yeah, I know.``
Incredible...
...My friends really are... the absolute best...
We're getting closer and closer to the outline of Takano's motive...
``...Ah, au au!@k@vS13/12/DS41070163. So then, what would this villain actually do?``

``Well, the first thing she'd do is petition her superiors!@k@vS13/03/DS41030117. She'd go to 'Tokyo' and argue that the disease she's researching is incredibly important and fascinating!@k@vS13/03/DS41030118. She'd plead with them to reverse the decision to shut it down.``
``But of course, if 'Tokyo' just said 'Sure, carry on, here's more funding,' there wouldn't be much of a manga!``
``Heh heh heh!@k@vS13/03/DS41030121. That's where the bosses at 'Tokyo' would thoroughly shut her down and tear her apart!``
``They'd say her research is worthless, a waste of the budget, and tell her to end it immediately, something truly harsh like that!``
Irie had said Takano went to Tokyo seeking an extension of her research.
...And that she was turned down.

``Oh ho ho!@k@vS13/04/DS41040075. If someone's life's work were mocked and dismissed, their fury would be something fearsome!@k@vS13/04/DS41040076. In that case, there are only two paths open to her!``
``...S-Satoko.@k@vS13/05/DS41050182. What would they be?``
``Isn't it obvious?!@k@vS13/01/DS41010104. Either swallow it in silence, or take revenge!``
Keiichi and Satoko slapped their palms together in a high five.
``Well, just taking it lying down wouldn't make for very exciting story!``
``So the villain who loved and cherished her research more than anyone starts thinking desperately: if someone else is going to destroy her life's work, she might as well end it herself!``
``All because of her pride!``
``Oh ho ho ho!@k@vS13/04/DS41040078. And that's where the agenda of someone within 'Tokyo' enters the picture!``
``...N-No way!@k@vS13/12/DS41070165. You mean that idea about someone profiting when the village is thrown into catastrophe and someone else is forced to take the blame...!``

``Exactly!!@k@vS13/04/DS41040080. The villain is depressed after having her life's work rejected.@k@vS13/04/DS41040081. Stoke those flames the right way, and you get a village-wide catastrophe without ever dirtying your own hands!``
``Then you can stroll in afterward and join the blame game at your leisure! How delicious!@k@vS13/04/DS41040083. See?``
``That's exactly it!@k@vS13/03/DS41030127. Heh heh heh!@k@vS13/03/DS41030128. And here's the thing.@k@vS13/03/DS41030129. This villain is in despair after having her life's work denied, right?``
``Then one of the ambitious young executives within 'Tokyo,' plotting to topple a rival and climb the ranks, dispatches an agent to ensnare this villain.``
``...Her very reason for existing has been trampled on, right?``
``...Then if someone tells her 'I believe in your research, keep going,' she might fall for it all too easily.@k@vS13/02/DS41020100. ...The stronger a person's pride, the more fragile they become.``
changeReason: Pronoun and framing changed: 'he believes in her' (third-person, gendered) to direct quote 'I believe in your research' (first-person); JPN uses 私は信じてるよ which is first-person 'I believe'.

TextENGNew: ``So what you're saying is, even the villain is just being used by an even dirtier puppet master.``
``Yeah, you see that kind of character in action shows all the time!``
``They slave away for the evil organization their whole life, but after one failure, they're tossed aside and eliminated just like that!``
``Actually, in this kind of scheme, eliminating the researcher who carried out the plan is absolutely essential, isn't it?@k@vS13/04/DS41040085. For the puppet master, she has no value alive!``
``Heh heh heh!@k@vS13/03/DS41030132. That's how it goes.``

``The villain might fancy herself a bit of a rogue, but in the end, her life's work is mocked, she's exploited by the puppet master, and finally silenced to cover their tracks. Even though she's the villain, you can't help but feel a bit sorry for her.``

``Well, that kind of character development has its appeal, don't you think?@k@|@y@vS13/03/DS41030135. ...No, actually, it definitely does, right?@k@vS13/03/DS41030136. Maybe I should try it in the next issue...``
......Hanyuu and I could only listen in stunned amazement.
Incredible.
They really are incredible.
It was entirely plausible, and knowing Takano's personality, it didn't seem impossible at all.
...Come to think of it, Irie had been repeating 'impossible' and 'absurd' an awful lot.
...Which, in a way, might actually be a reflection of just how possible he feared it might be.
If anyone could fill in the gaps around my questions, it was these friends of mine, right here and now.
changeReason: Old simplifies the metaphor 疑問の外堀を埋められる (fill in the surrounding aspects of my doubts) to 'can answer that question'; new preserves the investigative nuance.

TextENGNew: ...I should ask them, I need to ask them...!
``...Au.@k@vS13/12/DS41070167. Then why would she grow desperate now, when she still has three years left?``
``The remaining time isn't the point.@k@vS13/01/DS41010109. Whether you're executed today or three days from now, it's exactly the same, right?``
``It's just a matter of how that window feels.@k@vS13/01/DS41010111. The villain doesn't see it as three years of grace. She sees it as a death sentence to be carried out in three years.``

``That timing is probably dictated by the puppet masters.@k@vS13/03/DS41030138. They're the ones manipulating the villain, after all.@k@vS13/03/DS41030139. The reason the village needs to be destroyed at this particular time lies with the puppet masters, not the villain.``

``...After all, this is a massive operation.@k@vS13/03/DS41030141. Once it's set in motion, failure is not an option.@k@vS13/03/DS41030142. And the key to success isn't just assassinating the queen.@k@vS13/03/DS41030143. After the assassination, they have to get whoever's at the top to authorize the emergency manual.``
``On top of preparation for the post-operation blame game, there's also, well--@k@|@y@vS13/03/DS41030145. choosing the most effective timing to inflict maximum damage!``
``After weighing all those factors, they've decided that now, with three years still remaining, is the time to execute the grand operation, right?``
``So in the end, the villain is being used in every possible way, and when it's over, she'll be cut off like a lizard's tail.@k@vS13/01/DS41010113. Villain or not, you almost feel sorry for her.``
``...N-Now, let me move to the most important part.@k@vS13/05/DS41050184. To fight back against all this, how should the queen carrier girl fight?@k@vS13/05/DS41050185. She's the protagonist.``
``Oh!@k@vS13/01/DS41010115. Now that sounds exciting!@k@vS13/01/DS41010116. So the girl whose life is in danger just needs to somehow escape their clutches, right?``

``That depends on the format.@k@vS13/03/DS41030148. For a long-running series, you'd want her to keep evading them, with new pursuers and assassins showing up one after another so you never run out of material.``
``...Au, if possible, a short and focused serial would be best.``
Pfft.
I couldn't help but burst out laughing at Hanyuu's metaphor.
``In that case, there's no choice but to take down the villain and aim for a happy ending.@k@vS13/01/DS41010118. A story about fighting is way more exciting than one about running!``
``But the protagonist is just an ordinary girl, right?@k@vS13/04/DS41040087. How can she fight?``
``Heh heh heh!@k@|@y@vS13/03/DS41030150. Today's heroines aren't powerless, you know!``

``She might look like a demure, innocent girl on the surface,@k@|@y@vS13/03/DS41030152. but she has hidden super powers,@k@|@y@vS13/03/DS41030153. she's actually an S-rank hunter of royal blood,@k@|@y@vS13/03/DS41030154. and her alias is the Ebony Fallen Angel! How about that!!``
``Hauu.@k@vS13/02/DS41020102. That sounds like the kind of cringe-worthy OC a middle schooler would make up.``
``...What's wrong, Mion?@k@vS13/01/DS41010120. Got a stomachache or something?``
``Urgh... I-I guess this old man is just an egg that'll never hatch...``
...Mion, hang in there.
If you read that manga ten years from now, you'll probably writhe in sweet, sweet agony...
``...Au au.@k@vS13/12/DS41070172. I don't quite understand what Mion is saying, but anyway, the protagonist is a fragile girl with no special powers or finishing moves.``
``...If I had to name a strength... ......she's very good at putting on an innocent act.@k@vS13/12/DS41070174. Also she's quite mean, and she often torments me with spicy food.``
``...What a fine character trait.@k@vS13/05/DS41050187. I'll start putting that into practice tonight.``
``Au au au au au!!``
``I see, so she's just a regular girl. Got it.@k@vS13/01/DS41010122. ...But a story where a character like that triumphs over this kind of conspiracy... that's a tough nut to crack.``
``No way, no way, absolutely no way!@k@vS13/03/DS41030157. At bare minimum, she'd need proficiency with all types of weapons, radio operation skills, and the ability to pilot a helicopter!``

``Either that, or she'd have to be a survival specialist who trained under a foreign military advisory group.@k@vS13/03/DS41030159. Someone who could slip into the jungle and throw an entire enemy platoon into chaos with just a handful of weapons and traps!``
``...I have a feeling that if Mion and Satoko were friends with the protagonist, that alone would solve the problem.``
``Huh?@k@vS13/03/DS41030161. This old man?!``
``...Heh heh heh heh!@k@vS13/03/DS41030163. Well, if I were in the picture, that does change things considerably.``

``My combat skills and tactical command are both S-rank.@k@vS13/03/DS41030165. Plus, if this village is the setting for the manga,@k@vS13/03/DS41030166. my knowledge of the terrain is S-rank too!@k@vS13/03/DS41030167. And making an enemy of Satoko on top of that? That's absolutely fatal for them.@k@vS13/03/DS41030168. Right?``
``I don't know what kind of secret unit this villain commands!@k@vS13/04/DS41040089. But they're nothing against my trap expertise!``
``If the protagonist barricaded herself in the hills behind us with me, I wouldn't let a single finger touch her!``
``Yeah.@k@vS13/03/DS41030170. If she holes up with Satoko in the hills, the attackers would need naval bombardment capable of gouging out the entire mountain, plus heavy aerial bombing at minimum.``
``Even after all that, they'd still suffer heavy casualties trying to push into the hills.``

``And with me commanding the operations!@k@|@y@vS13/03/DS41030173. ...Well, the attacking force had better be prepared to lose more than half their strength!@k@vS13/03/DS41030174. Heh heh heh, right, Satoko?``
``Oh ho ho ho ho!!@k@vS13/04/DS41040092. They'd better bring a nuclear warhead if they want to crack my artistic mountain fortress!!``
``...True, Satoko in the hills would be formidable, but... can we really defeat the Mountain D-- au, the villain's henchmen?@k@vS13/12/DS41070178. They're professionals at violence, you know...?``
``Surprise attacks and traps are effective against anyone, regardless of skill.@k@vS13/03/DS41030176. History is full of examples proving this.``
``Cutting-edge armies equipped with the finest gear have been defeated time and again in jungles and mountains, unable to bring their capabilities to bear.``
``Traps set in layers by someone who knows every inch of the terrain possess a fearsome defensive power that no veteran would ever underestimate.``
``...You're saying Satoko's traps would work against even them...?``

``Absolutely!@k@vS13/03/DS41030180. In fact, precisely because they're professionals, the effect might be even greater.@k@vS13/03/DS41030181. Traps aren't just about catching people.@k@vS13/03/DS41030182. The fear of possibly being caught has enormous psychological impact too.``

``Being professionals, they'll proceed with extra caution.@k@vS13/03/DS41030184. Their commander will hesitate to order a direct assault into the hills.@k@vS13/03/DS41030185. That means we buy more time than we would against amateurs.``
``Heh heh!@k@vS13/01/DS41010124. So they'd need at least naval bombardment to break through.@k@vS13/01/DS41010125. But this isn't wartime, so surely the enemy can't go that far?``
......Surely, they couldn't take it that far.
In any case, Mion is exceptionally professional when it comes to tactical judgment.
Having her vouch that Satoko's traps would be more than effective even against the Mountain Dogs was enormously reassuring.

``The weaknesses of traps are that they can only be used defensively, they take time to set up,@k@vS13/03/DS41030187. and they trigger indiscriminately. But on all those points, Satoko's hills meet every condition perfectly.``

``Th-That's true.@k@vS13/02/DS41020104. Nobody ever goes into those hills, and they're already packed with Satoko-chan's traps.@k@vS13/02/DS41020105. Being able to barricade herself just by running there would be a huge advantage for the protagonist.``

``If I'm going to be there too, I won't just use it as a simple barricade!@k@vS13/03/DS41030189. I'll use it as a diversion to draw the enemy's main force in, then strike at their flank with a surprise attack!``

``Luring the enemy deep in and hitting them from behind when they're overextended is the ABCs of counter-ambush tactics.@k@vS13/03/DS41030191. Trying to hold the shoreline at all costs is the height of rigidity!``
``Hauuu!@k@vS13/02/DS41020107. Mii-chan and Satoko-chan are amazing...!@k@|@y@vS13/02/DS41020108. But, hau, Rena and Keiichi-kun are being left out...``
``...Then let's say we're all the protagonists.@k@vS13/12/DS41070180. We'll fight them off together, as friends.``
``All right!!@k@vS13/01/DS41010127. Now that's what I'm talking about!@k@vS13/01/DS41010128. Heh heh, so with all of us joining in, it's basically the club members versus an evil organization, huh?``

``...Heh heh heh heh, hee hee hee hee!@k@vS13/03/DS41030193. I don't know what kind of villain this is, but they're out of luck.@k@vS13/03/DS41030194. I don't know the size of their force, but heh heh heh!``

``A head-on clash would be one thing, but if they let us use our knowledge of the terrain, they can't win in this village.@k@vS13/03/DS41030196. If they want to beat us in Hinamizawa, they'd need an operation on the scale of Overlord!``
``Though even that, our club would repel!@k@vS13/03/DS41030198. Heh heh heh!``
When Mion flashed everyone a confident smile, they all nodded back.
...Incredible. If our club pulls out all the stops, I'm actually starting to believe we could hold our own even against the Mountain Dogs.
In a straight fight, sure, we'd be overwhelmed in an instant even with a surprise attack.
...But if we're the ones setting the stage, and if our preparations are thorough enough... all bets are off.
``...See, Rika?@k@vS13/12/DS41070182. Your friends are incredibly dependable.``
``......You're absolutely right.@k@vS13/05/DS41050190. ...Why on earth didn't I confide in such reliable allies until now?``
``...What you can't do alone, you can accomplish with everyone together.``
Things that one person alone would need a miracle to achieve... with everyone working together, could be accomplished with surprising ease.
...In other words, that itself is a miracle.
I could feel something stirring with excitement deep in my chest.
changeReason: Old 'My heart is pounding' is ambiguous (could suggest fear/anxiety); new correctly specifies excitement, matching JPN わくわく (excitement/anticipation).

TextENGNew: ...What is this feeling?
With these friends beside me, I felt as though nothing was impossible.
...In a world I don't remember, the one Hanyuu told me about, they even overcame the worst fate of all, the world where Teppei returns.
...I don't remember it, and yet... something deep in my heart aches, telling me that the same miracle is about to unfold in this world too.
``...M-Mii.@k@vS13/05/DS41050192. And everyone.@k@vS13/05/DS41050193. ...I want even more ideas.@k@vS13/05/DS41050194. How can we defeat this conspiracy...? Please, tell me more...!``
